preamble.summary: Oracles against Ammon, Edom, Damascus, Kedar/Hazor, and Elam. MT ch. 49 bundles five oracles. In LXX these are SCATTERED across the OAN section with different numbering and a completely different ORDER.
preamble.notable_variants: LXX is shorter throughout, especially in the Edom oracle (vv. 7-22, which parallels Obadiah). The Elam oracle (vv. 34-39) is FIRST in LXX OAN order (LXX ch. 25:14ff) but EIGHTH in MT. The Damascus oracle is significantly shorter in LXX.
preamble.structural_notes: MT ch. 49 contains 5 sub-oracles with LXX equivalents: Ammon (MT 49:1-6 = LXX 30:1-5, 6th in LXX), Edom (MT 49:7-22 = LXX 29:7-22, 5th in LXX), Damascus (MT 49:23-27 = LXX 30:12-16, 8th in LXX), Kedar/Hazor (MT 49:28-33 = LXX 30:6-11, 7th in LXX), Elam (MT 49:34-39 = LXX 25:14-19, 1st in LXX).

variant_notes: Ammon oracle. LXX = ch. 30:1-5 (6th OAN). Minor differences from MT.

verses.mt_reading: אֲשֶׁר הָיָה דְבַר־יְהוָה אֶל־יִרְמְיָהוּ הַנָּבִיא אֶל עֵילָם
verses.lxx_reading: ὃ ἐγένετο ῥῆμα κυρίου πρὸς Ιερεμιαν τὸν προφήτην ἐπὶ Αιλαμ
verses.variant_rendering: The word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet against Elam.
verses.mt_rendering: The word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam.
variant_notes: Elam oracle begins. In LXX, this is the FIRST Oracle Against the Nations (LXX 25:14ff), placed immediately after the ch. 25 transition. In MT, Elam is the 8th oracle (near the end). LXX's placement of Elam first is one of the most distinctive features of the LXX arrangement.
Elam oracle. Substantially identical in content between MT and LXX.
